news.Title: 80 Billion Reasons to Buy Nvidia After Its Monster Earnings Report
news.Content:
As earnings season comes to a close, artificial intelligence (AI) investors eagerly awaited Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) to report its first-quarter results last Wednesday. I wasn't too surprised to learn that the company once again blew past Wall Street's expectations and also beat its own internal guidance.

After all, the rest of big tech -- many of which are Nvidia's largest customers -- already reported earnings. The main theme from those reports was that investment in AI infrastructure is accelerating. That's a good sign for Nvidia's core data center business.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

What I most certainly did not expect was the company's announcement of an additional $80 billion stock repurchase authorization. Let's explore what a stock buyback signals about management's views regarding the current AI capex supercycle.

Why do companies buy back stock?

If management believes its company's stock is trading below its intrinsic value , it may choose to deploy excess capital to buy back shares. Moreover, buybacks often reflect management's confidence in the company's ability to sustain robust growth. Rather than hoard cash or pursue questionable acquisitions, companies can return capital to shareholders when they are optimistic about the revenue and profit expansion ahead.

In Nvidia's case, with massive free cash flow amid unrelenting chip demand from hyperscalers, such shareholder rewards programs underscore management's belief that the company's trajectory outpaces current market pricing.

What does Nvidia's share repurchase history look like?

In August 2023, the company authorized a $25 billion buyback without expiration. Of note, this was an increase to a prior buyback program. Exactly one year later, Nvidia approved a subsequent $50 billion stock repurchase program. This was followed by the announcement of an additional $60 billion in buybacks in August 2025.

During fiscal 2026 (period ended Jan. 25), Nvidia returned over $41.1 billion to shareholders through a combination of buybacks and dividends. Entering the latest quarter with $38.5 billion remaining under the prior buyback program, Nvidia's board of directors approved another $80 billion share repurchase without expiration.

Nvidia stock looks dirt cheap right now

A forward price-to-earnings (P/E) analysis further illustrates why Nvidia's latest buyback may reflect astute timing. With Nvidia currently trading at 25 times next year's expected earnings, Nvidia's multiple appears quite modest relative to the premiums it commanded during similar expansion phases throughout the AI revolution.

Story Continues

I think this discount stems from a number of overlooked catalysts beyond the core data center GPU business. Inference workloads are exploding as enterprises deploy AI at scale -- demanding more compute resources alongside Nvidia's GPUs.

In addition, agentic AI represents a new frontier. Autonomous agents require enormous inference cycles and CPU horsepower, thereby creating layered demand that complements Nvidia's training GPUs. As hyperscalers shift to include CPUs -- such as Nvidia's Vera architecture -- alongside existing GPU clusters, the company stands to become a key supplier in hybrid GPU-CPU systems.

At the end of the day, Nvidia's latest buyback isn't just a simple capital return program. It's a strong vote of confidence that the stock's growth trajectory is poised to kick into a new gear as the AI infrastructure era takes off.

news.Title: Move Over, AST SpaceMobile: Alphabet Has a Surprising New No. 1 Holding... and It's From the Financial Sector
news.Content:
In case you missed it, one of the most important data releases of the quarter hit the newswires on May 15: Form 13F filings . These filings provide investors with a way to track which stocks Wall Street's savviest fund managers and businesses bought and sold in the latest quarter.

Google parent Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG) is one company that's required to file quarterly 13Fs for its $4 billion investment portfolio. While satellite-based cellular broadband services provider AST SpaceMobile (NASDAQ: ASTS) had been Alphabet's No. 1 holding in each of the three previous quarters , this isn't the case any longer. It's been unseated by financial services giant CME Group (NASDAQ: CME) .

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly," providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need.

Continue »

AST SpaceMobile still makes up more than 18% of Alphabet's $4 billion portfolio

Although AST was dethroned as Alphabet's largest investment holding, Google's parent didn't sell a share (it still holds 8,943,486 shares). This likely has to do with AST's two core catalysts remaining firmly in place.

First, its BlueBird satellites work with existing smartphone technology. Whereas prior-generation satellite-based cellular networks required specialized devices, AST's network is ready to roll with what consumers are already using.

Secondly, AST SpaceMobile has secured partnerships with more than 50 global mobile network providers. Instead of fighting an uphill battle against established wireless providers to earn subscribers, it's partnering with them, giving AST access to 5.8 billion combined subscribers. In theory, this should lead to a rapid sales ramp as its satellite-based cellular broadband network expands.

At the same time, AST's operating model is capital-intensive and has little margin for error. Higher satellite production costs and launch delays can adversely affect a stock that's soared by more than 1,800% over the trailing two years.

Alphabet takes a billion-dollar stake in CME Group

While Alphabet exited six positions in the first quarter and opened three others, none was more of an eyebrow-raiser than the 3,484,020 shares purchased of financial services company CME Group -- a stake worth $1.03 billion as of March 31.

As is typical, Alphabet almost exclusively invests in companies with which it already has working partnerships. Google announced an equity investment of $1 billion in nonvoting shares of convertible, preferred CME Group stock in November 2021. The duo also forged a 10-year partnership that's seen CME rely on Google Cloud to host its trading system.

Story Continues

Arguably, the biggest lure of CME Group for investors is the dominance and uniqueness of its financial derivatives trading platforms (futures and option contracts). CME accounts for approximately 90% of U.S. futures market share and is also the undisputed global leader in equity index futures. This sustainable moat has been a multidecade tailwind for CME, with average daily volume reaching an all-time high of 36.2 million contracts in the first quarter.

But CME Group's margins are no slouch, either. The beauty of technology-driven trading platforms is that they're asset-light, leading to double-digit sales growth in most quarters and a gross margin that's trended into the mid-80% range.

CME Group is leading the cloud-based and AI-driven transformation of the financial services space, and Alphabet stands to benefit as both an investor and the company whose AI-fueled cloud infrastructure service platform is powering this shift.

news.Title: 鉅亨投資雷達》AI出口動能延續，多家基金公司上修2026年台股展望
news.Content: 根據行政院主計總處統計，台灣 2026 年第一季經濟成長率達 13.7%，為近 39 年來最佳表現，出口年增率更是較 2025 年同期成長 51.1%。此數據增長不僅帶動台灣企業獲利飆升，更使台股屢創新高。但在今年以來地緣政治風險不斷的情況下，台股是否能一直保持強勢？ 1. 台灣 2026 年經濟增長展現強勁動能 隨著全球 AI 應用進入爆發期，台灣作為核心供應鏈的地位更加穩固。台灣經濟研究院預估，2026 年台灣全年經濟成長率將達 7.6%，出口年增率高達 27.1%，分別較前次預測上修 3.6% 及 13.3%。主要動能來自全球 AI 基礎建設投資持續擴大，帶動高階伺服器、半導體與資通訊產業需求強勁，加上電子零組件與原物料供給吃緊，推升供應鏈價格，形成量價齊揚的成長態勢。根據市場預期，2026 年超大規模雲端服務商（CSP）的資本支出將達 7,743 億美元，也使各大基金公司同步上修台灣經濟成長率至 7% 至 8.7%，顯示看好台灣經濟前景已逐漸成為市場共識。 2. 出口擴張驅動台股獲利，預期 2026 年企業每股盈餘成長幅度大 台灣經濟成長動能主要來自強勁出口，而這也直接反映在基金公司對台股獲利的樂觀看法上。從各家預估來看， 2026 年台股企業每股盈餘（EPS）年增率多落在 19.7% 至 50% 之間 ，其中鉅亨買基金預估為 30%，顯示我們同樣看好 AI 需求延續、出口擴張與台灣供應鏈競爭力對企業獲利的支撐。整體而言，隨著全球 AI 基礎建設投資持續擴大，台灣在半導體、伺服器與電子零組件等關鍵領域具備高度優勢，基金公司普遍預期 2026 年台股企業獲利仍將維持雙位數成長，後續若企業獲利持續上修，將有望進一步支撐台股表現。 3. 基金公司展望： 2026 年台股仍具強勁成長動能 展望 2026 年，基金公司普遍看好台灣經濟與股市發展，在 AI 出口需求的強勁支撐下，兩者皆展現高度成長韌性。儘管地緣政治與中東戰事引發短期盤勢震盪，但台廠憑藉全球 AI 供應鏈的關鍵地位與技術優勢，基本面依舊穩健。 鉅亨買基金認為，台灣出口動能強勁是支撐台灣經濟的重要引擎，若 AI 資本支出與出口動能延續，台股供應鏈仍有機會持續受惠；但仍須留意地緣政治、利率與匯率風險。（詳細請參閱 四萬點只是起點？AI 需求擴張撐起台股長線行情 ）

news.Title: 3 Reasons Nvidia Still Has Plenty of Room to Run
news.Content:
Although Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) may be the world's most valuable company, I think the stock still has a lot of room to rise. The market may have come to expect a lot from Nvidia, but there is a major growth runway ahead for the stock, and I think it's among the best buys right now.

I've got three reasons Nvidia can continue rising, and all of them add up to make the stock a genius buy right now.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

1. Vera Rubin's revenue hasn't even come in the door yet

Nvidia tends to launch a new chip architecture every few years. The upgrades from one generation to the next are incredible, and that's exactly what we'll see when the Vera Rubin architecture is implemented, replacing the current Blackwell technology. Rubin chips can run inference 10 times cheaper than a comparable Blackwell chip, and four times less for training. That's a major upgrade that will unlock new AI capabilities that we haven't seen yet.

While Nvidia has been hyping this launch, it has recorded no revenue from the platform. However, by the third quarter of this year, Vera Rubin chips will start shipping, and that could unlock a whole new venue of revenue growth for Nvidia. There's still a lot more ahead for Nvidia , and that makes me bullish as an investor.

2. The data-center build-out trend is set to last for many more years

One item that may be holding investors up are doubts about how long the data-center build-out will last. After all, of Nvidia's $81.6 billion in Q1 revenue (up 85% year over year), $75.2 billion came from its data-center division. That's a major concentration, but when this side of the business is booming, Nvidia would be unwise not to pursue it on a full scale. Although there will likely be some slowdown in data-center spending eventually, we're nowhere near seeing that happen.

One of Nvidia's largest clients, Alphabet , has already told investors that 2027 capital expenditures will be "significantly higher" than they were in 2026. That shows that the AI build-out will at least extend into 2027, but Nvidia believes there's even more to come.

Nvidia told investors during its Q1 earnings conference call that 2027 hyperscaler capital expenditures will exceed $1 trillion. By 2030, that figure might to grow to $3 trillion to $4 trillion. That bodes well for Nvidia, and the growth that it would experience if that pans out would be remarkable.

Story Continues

3. The market isn't pricing in any growth after this year

Right now, Nvidia trades for about 27 times forward earnings.

That means that as long as Nvidia hits growth projections (which it has a long track record of exceeding), it would trade for a mere 27 times trailing earnings, as long as the stock price stays the same. However, with major growth projections like the one Nvidia gave for 2027 capital expenditures, plus revenue boosts from Vera Rubin, I think it's safe to say that 2027 will be another year of remarkable growth for Nvidia. Even if Nvidia's growth slows way down to something like 50% year over year, buying a stock that trades at 27 times earnings and is growing that quickly is a no-brainer.

The market is fairly short-sighted and usually only cares about the next year. By buying Nvidia stock now, you're getting on board a multiyear growth trajectory.

news.Title: 黃仁勳掀AI狂潮助台股攻克4萬3 市值型ETF爆發 009802、00905帶頭衝
news.Content: 受惠美伊談判釋出正面訊號、美債殖利率走低，加上輝達執行長黃仁勳來台掀起 AI 熱潮，台股今 (25) 日多頭氣勢全面升溫，加權指數終場大漲 1,376.43 點，收在 43,644.40 點，改寫歷史收盤新高，成交值放大至 1 兆 3,013 億元。在權王台積電領軍，以及聯發科、聯電雙雙亮燈漲停帶動下，外資現貨買超 495 億元，也推升指標權值 ETF 元大台灣 50 收在 100.80 元，正式站上百元大關，股價 30 元的市值型 ETF009802 及 00905 帶頭衝，本月份漲幅分居一二。 隨著台股屢創新高，價格相對親民、適合小資族布局的 30 元以下台股市值型 ETF，表現同樣受到市場關注。 根據 CMoney 統計，今日 30 元以下漲幅前五大台股市值型 ETF 中，除了近期備受矚目的主動式 ETF 外，具備 Smart Beta 選股邏輯的指數型 ETF 也成功突圍，顯示在多頭行情中，並非只有主動式商品能夠掌握行情，透過因子篩選與指數化紀律布局的 ETF，同樣具備參與漲勢與爭取超額報酬的能力。 觀察今日漲幅排行，富邦旗艦 50 以 7.15% 的單日漲幅居冠，收在 19.64 元；主動第一金台股優、主動國泰動能高息及主動群益台灣強棒分別上漲 5.27%、4.82% 與 4.77%。此外，FT 臺灣 Smart 也上漲 4.53%，收在 26.55 元。若拉長期間觀察，富邦旗艦 50 今年以來及近一年漲幅分別達 74.42%、108.49%；FT 臺灣 Smart 則達 52.59%、104.07%，顯示低價 ETF 在本波多頭行情中累積漲幅相當可觀。 值得意的是，榜單中除主動式 ETF 表現亮眼外，富邦旗艦 50 與 FT 臺灣 Smart 兩檔具 Smart Beta 成分的指數型 ETF 也同步進榜。進一步觀察成分配置，富邦旗艦 50 並未納入台積電，漲勢主要反映其他權值股與成分股輪動；FT 臺灣 Smart 則將台積電權重維持在約 30% 左右，能參與本波由半導體與 AI 族群領軍的主流行情，同時透過多因子選股機制分散至其他具動能、品質或市值特色的標的，有助降低單一個股集中風險。 展望後市，台股在先前創下 42,408 點前高後一度回測月線，所幸在 4 萬點大關獲得支撐；如今在外資回補、融資追價力道轉強，以及蘇姿丰與黃仁勳相繼來台帶動新一波 AI 題材發酵下，指數走勢明顯優於市場預期。 法人認為，電子指數開高走高並續創新高，缺口未補下仍屬強勢格局，本波行情有機會進一步挑戰 45,000 至 46,000 點。不過指數急漲後震盪恐加劇，投資人仍宜留意追高風險，並可透過具 Smart Beta 機制與分散配置特色的 ETF，以紀律化方式參與台股多頭行情。 30 元以下台股市值型 ETF 漲幅前五強 股票代號 股票名稱 收盤價 (元) 今日漲幅 (%) 本月漲幅 (%) 今年以來漲幅 (%) 近一年漲幅 (%)

news.Title: 打入台積電供應鏈 華洋精機上櫃大漲逾3倍
news.Content:
[ 非凡新聞 ]記者華語涵

新興顯示技術迎來爆發性成長！根據國際報告預估，全球平面顯示器檢測設備市場，將在2035年上看11.43億美元；華洋精機搭上這股趨勢，今（25）日以每股承銷價85元掛牌上櫃，開盤就衝上349.5元，漲幅超過3倍，展現強勁的甜蜜行情！

熱情的舞獅表演，搭配陣陣鼓聲，為典禮增添喜慶氛圍！以半導體自動化光學檢測起家的華洋精機，以每股承銷價85元正式掛牌上櫃，業者也看好先進封裝強勁的成長動能，趁勢進軍資本市場。

華洋精機董事長 邱見泰：「我們會開發一些，所謂的量檢測設備，我們也會跟一些國際的一些光學大廠做合作，做到不管是在半導體的前段，或者是中段，或後段的部分的，這些所有的設備，會因應國內半導體的蓬勃發展，而去做客戶最想要、最需要的設備。」

業者指出，公司從2012年成立至今，憑藉豐富的光學設計、精密演算法，以及客製化製程的整合能力，開發出的「表面光學去背景」技術，不需要透過軟體大量過濾，就能精準辨識出微小瑕疵，也成為公司的一大競爭優勢。

華洋精機總經理 蕭賢德：「我們會跟一些光學大廠，一起做一些，未來會做一些檢測設備，給我們客戶用，提供更高階，解析度更高的解決方案給客戶，陸續會把一些比較有動能的，比如說AOI、AVI、AXI，還有AOM，這些我們都會提供很完整的解決方案給客戶。」

目前業者已經擁有15項發明專利、12項新型專利；展望未來，除了聚焦先進封裝與新材料以外，也會持續推展到晶圓製程領域。業者也積極佈局海外，第一步率先搶進日本、新加坡、韓國市場，目標在國際供應鏈中，佔有一席之地！（記者 華語涵、攝影 柯思安／台北採訪報導）

news.Title: 台股收43644點新高 分析師：輝達兆元宴、COMPUTEX助攻
news.Content:
受中東戰事有望好轉、AI概念股推波助瀾，台北股市今(25)日盤中創歷史新高，登上43645點，終場收在43644點，為收盤新高紀錄，大漲1376點，漲幅3.26%。分析師認為，中東停戰堪稱最後利多，為台股帶來刺激效果僅為短期，而這兩週受到輝達「兆元宴」、台北國際電腦展(COMPUTEX)開展等題材加持，台股雖有望持續攻高，但也需留意回檔壓力恐隨之而來。

中東戰事將滿一季之際，市場傳出美伊停戰協議有望達成，國際油價應聲滑落，再加上台北國際電腦展6月登場在即，超微(AMD)宣布將在台投資逾100億美元，執行長蘇姿丰日前在台強調「現在是擴大投資台灣供應鏈的最佳時機」，隨後輝達(NVIDIA)執行長黃仁勳23日也抵台，規劃27日在北士科T17、T18基地舉辦員工大會，28日將舉辦「兆元宴」，備受各界矚目。

在中東地緣政治有望緩解，AI科技業題材加持下，台股25日氣勢如虹，權值股包括台積電、聯發科領軍帶領大盤向上衝高，尤其聯發科一路漲停鎖死，加權指數一度來到43645點，創盤中新高紀錄，終場則以大漲1376點、歷史第五大漲點，收在43644點，創收盤新高紀錄，漲幅3.26%。

資深分析師蔡明翰指出，美伊和談有望，荷莫茲海峽有機會重新開放，可使維持在高檔的國際油價鬆動，進一步舒緩美國通膨壓力，對美國經濟、股市都是利多，不過，4月以來，股市對美伊戰事反應逐漸鈍化，荷莫茲海峽開放的利多激勵將屬短期，且利多出盡。

蔡明翰表示，按照過去經驗，輝達「兆元宴」、台北國際電腦展等題材，都能在短期內助攻台股持續向上衝高，不過，投資人也得留意漲多回檔的風險。他說：『(原音)這個禮拜的兆元宴對市場來講，會有信心的激勵作用，中長線AI的需求非常強勁，這個趨勢就近期的角度還是有辦法持續獲得確認。但是短線漲多之後出現震盪也是在所難免的，所以要提醒投資人，中長線還是要維持樂觀思維。』

匯市部分，新台幣兌美元匯價今天收在31.47元，升9.8分。(編輯：宋皖媛)

news.Title: Huawei Touts Chip Breakthrough to Shorten Gap With TSMC
news.Content:
(Bloomberg) -- Huawei Technologies Co. said it has come up with a new pathway to shorten its gap with industry leader Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., potentially achieving a breakthrough in making advanced semiconductors without cutting-edge equipment.

Most Read from Bloomberg

Nvidia CEO Urges Super Micro to Tighten Up on Compliance

Trump Doctor Visit Renews Health Scrutiny as 80th Birthday Nears

Trump Says He’ll Announce Negotiated Deal With Iran Shortly

Rubio Sees Good News Coming on Hormuz as Iran Talks Continue

US, Iran Inch Toward Deal as Gaps Remain on Uranium, Sanctions

Right now there is about a five-year gap between what TSMC is capable of and what Huawei together with its manufacturing partner Semiconductor Manufacturing International Corp. can produce. Huawei will start making 1.4-nanometer chips by 2031 with its own “LogicFolding” technology, Huawei’s semiconductor chief He Tingbo said in a rare public appearance during a chip conference on Monday, while TSMC has said that it will begin mass production of the same product in 2028.

The executive said that her team has found a way for “sustainable evolution.” She told reporters after her speech on Monday that Huawei can advance its chipmaking prowess significantly without the use of Dutch supplier ASML Holding NV’s extreme ultraviolet lithography machines, widely considered as essential for production of cutting-edge semiconductors that China doesn’t have access to.

He added that the Kirin mobile chips to be launched this fall will be the first to adopt the LogicFolding architecture, which helps boost performance of a chip by increasing the number of transistors it carries and optimizing data transmission speed.

“This year we have prepared a surprise for the whole industry. Not saturation, not continuation, but a big leap ahead,” she said.

The Star 50 Index in Shanghai, which includes several major Chinese chip firms, rose to a record after He’s announcement on Monday. Shares of SMIC rose more than 18% while foundry peer Hua Hong Semiconductor Ltd. surged by the daily limit of 20%.

If Huawei can manage to make 1.4nm semiconductors in large quantities, it means it’s defying the industry consensus that ASML’s EUV lithography machines are necessary to mass-produce chips that are 5nm or more advanced. Such semiconductors are used to power the most sophisticated AI technologies.

The nanometer measure is used to indicate the size of transistors on a chip. The smaller a transistor becomes, the more can be fitted on a chip, which in turn will become more powerful. ASML’s EUV machines are seen as essential in shrinking transistors, and they are used widely by leading global chipmakers including TSMC, Samsung Electronics Co. and Intel Corp. for mass production.

Story Continues

Huawei’s He added that the LogicFolding architecture is based on Huawei’s own Tau Scaling Law, a principle the Chinese company is adopting to rival Moore’s Law, the guideline for the global chip industry for decades. Moore’s Law, named after Intel co-founder Gordon Moore, is an extrapolation that the numbers of transistors in a chip will roughly double about every two years, though many in the industry including He say the cadence has been slowing down in recent years.

He said that Huawei’s scaling efforts based on Moore’s Law plateaued six years ago after US export controls hit. Her team then proposed a new “time scaling” method to replace the global industry’s gold standard. Huawei’s new principle appears to focus on boosting data transmission speed by transistors to compensate for a lack of state-of-the-art equipment to shrink those components significantly further.

“We saw time scaling can deliver strong benefits across devices, circuits, chips and systems,” He said, adding that Huawei has designed and made 381 chips over the past six years based on the Tau Scaling Law. Huawei in a separate statement said that the company is dubbing its new principle “Her’s Law,” in a nod to its chip chief.

The Tau Scaling Law is a summary of some current trends in the semiconductor industry, but it appears to be the first attempt by a company to formulate these ideas into a coherent theory, according to Kitty Fok, managing director of research firm IDC China.

“It may also provide a new reference point for China’s semiconductor industry in overcoming process-node constraints,” Fok said.

The Shenzhen firm has previously filed for patents that show it is trying so-called self-aligned quadruple patterning, or SAQP, that may allow it to produce advanced chips without ASML’s EUV machines. Quadruple patterning is a technique for etching lines on silicon wafers multiple times to increase transistor density — and therefore performance.

While Huawei has continued to improve its technologies over the past few years, it is unclear whether it will indeed be able to reach the cutting edge of chipmaking by experimenting with a non-mainstream path.

Huawei has been at the vanguard of Beijing’s semiconductor self-sufficiency drive following a years-long US-led, multinational campaign to tighten exports of advanced chips and gear that have somewhat curbed China’s AI progress.

In September, Huawei announced a three-year roadmap to roll out a series of AI chips to fill in the vacuum left by Nvidia Corp., whose most advanced semiconductors are banned for China.

--With assistance from Kelly Li.

(Adds Huawei executive’s comments from the third paragraph.)

Most Read from Bloomberg Businessweek

College Kids Don’t Want Your AI

Blame YouTube, Not Just Trump, for the End of Colbert’s Late Show

news.Title: Here Are My Top 3 High-Yield Dividend Stocks to Buy Now
news.Content:
My portfolio is chock-full of dividend stocks. Many of them offer high yields. By the way, I consider a yield as high if it's more than two times greater than the yield of the S&P 500 (SNPINDEX: ^GSPC) . This approach works better for me than going with a fixed percentage.

What are my top high-yield dividend stocks to buy now? Here's why Enbridge (NYSE: ENB) , Enterprise Products Partners (NYSE: EPD) , and Verizon Communications (NYSE: VZ) stand out.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

1. Enbridge

Enbridge pays a forward dividend yield of 4.9%, almost five times higher than the S&P 500's yield. In addition to this juicy yield, the company has increased its dividend for an impressive 31 consecutive years. Its distributable cash flow payout ratio of between 60% and 70% is also healthy.

Great dividends are the byproduct of great underlying businesses. Enbridge is a leader in the midstream energy industry. It operates the world's oil and liquids pipeline network, serving more than 75% of North American refineries. The company's pipelines also transport around 20% of all natural gas consumed in North America.

But Enbridge isn't only a pipeline stock . It ranks as the largest natural gas utility in North America by volume. The company has also expanded its renewable energy operations and now provides renewable power to top customers, including AT&T (NYSE: T) , Meta Platforms (NASDAQ: META) , and Toyota (NYSE: TM) .

Enbridge has clear visibility into growth through the end of the decade. North American liquid natural gas (LNG) demand is expected to increase to over 30 billion cubic feet per day by 2030, while gas demand is projected to increase to roughly 28 billion cubic feet per day. Management has identified around $50 billion of growth opportunities over the next four years to meet this rising demand.

2. Enterprise Products Partners

Enterprise Products Partners offers an even higher distribution yield of 5.6%. The limited partnership (LP) has increased its distribution for 27 consecutive years. It appears to be in great shape to keep the streak going, with a payout ratio of 57% based on adjusted cash flow from operations.

Like Enbridge, Enterprise Products Partners is a key player in the North American midstream energy market. It operates over 50,000 miles of pipeline that transport natural gas liquids (NGLs), crude oil, natural gas, petrochemicals, and other refined products.

Story Continues

I really like the LP's stability and financial strength. Enterprise Products Partners' solid balance sheet earned it the highest credit rating in the midstream industry. The company has generated steady cash flow over the last 20 years, even during challenging periods such as the 2008 to 2009 financial crisis and the COVID-19 pandemic.

Enterprise's growth prospects look good, too. Data centers running artificial intelligence (AI) applications are providing a tailwind for natural gas in the U.S. The demand for LNG in Asia and Europe is expected to grow by around 30% by 2030.

3. Verizon Communications

Verizon Communications currently pays a forward dividend yield of 5.9%. The company has increased its dividend for 19 consecutive years. Its dividend payout ratio is a comfortable 67%.

Of these three high-yield dividend stocks, Verizon is probably the most familiar to investors. Verizon ranks as the world's sixth-largest communications services company by market cap. It provides broadband and wireless services to millions of consumers and businesses.

I'm confident that Verizon can continue to pay its juicy dividends. Why? The company is generating strong and growing free cash flow. Verizon expects free cash flow of $21.5 billion in full-year 2026, up 7% year over year and reflecting the highest level since 2020.

I also think that Verizon's wireless services will enjoy strong demand for years to come. By 2030, 6G networks should be widely available throughout much of the U.S. I predict an explosion in new capabilities that will directly benefit Verizon.

news.Title: 統一期貨：聯發科等權值股領漲，台指期開高走高，攻上44,000整數關卡
news.Content:
【財訊快報／編輯部】週末川普表示美伊協議即將達成，荷姆茲海峽近期開放，帶動清晨油價重挫，NQ期創高上攻三萬點整數大關，也推升台指期週一開高逾700點。在聯發科、台達電等權值股點火領漲下，台指期開高走高，一度漲逾1,500點，攻上44,000整數關卡。綜觀法人籌碼，三大法人合計買超642.9億元，其中外資買超494.6億元，而自營

商買超68.9億元，投信則買超79.4億元。檢視選擇權金流，外資布局1.12億元偏多部

位，自營商則布局1.61億元偏空部位。選擇權未平倉部分，5月W4週選價平近9檔賣

權最大未平倉上升1,400點至43,400點，買權最大未平倉上升1,500點至44,000點，

最大OI區間上移偏多。期貨方面，外資夜盤加碼1,454口空單，日盤則大幅加碼4,181

口空單，未平倉淨空單增至-50,995口；十大特定法人加碼2,489口空單至-4,092口。

news.Title: Prediction: Alphabet Stock Is a Buy Before June 2026
news.Content:
Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOGL) (NASDAQ: GOOG) has done nothing but prove the bears wrong. The business, which critics previously thought had lost the artificial intelligence (AI) race, has produced a stellar return. Shares have skyrocketed roughly 130% just in the past 12 months (as of May 22).

Alphabet sports a colossal market cap of $4.6 trillion. This makes it the world's second-most-valuable enterprise, with Nvidia wearing the crown.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

For a company that has lifted the portfolios of its shareholder base in remarkable fashion, there is still a lot of gas left in the tank. The show goes on, making t his "Magnificent Seven" stock is a smart buy before June 2026.

The business is firing on all cylinders

Despite constantly being in the spotlight, Alphabet continues to pleasantly surprise the market. This is exactly what it did on April 29 when it reported first quarter earnings.

Revenue surged 22% year over year to $109.9 billion, which beat Wall Street analyst estimates. This was the fastest rate of growth in 16 quarters.

Revenue at Google Search rose 19%. "We see AI Overviews and AI Mode continue to drive greater Search usage and growth in overall queries, including in commercial queries," Chief Business Officer Philipp Schindler said on the Q1 earnings call.

Ad revenue for popular streaming platform YouTube rose by almost 11%. And Waymo, Alphabet's autonomous driving segment, is now completing more than 500,000 rides per week.

Investors can't forget about Google Cloud, which is the clear star. This segment is really hitting its stride. Revenue jumped 63% year over year, faster than the increases of rivals Amazon Web Services and Microsoft Azure.

Google Cloud ended the first quarter with $462 billion in customer backlogs , almost doubling from three months before. Demand for AI products and services is as robust as ever.

Huge spending does present a potential risk

Investors are all too familiar with the vast amount of spending being directed to the AI infrastructure build-out. Alphabet is one of the leaders in this regard. Its capital expenditures (capex) totaled $91 billion in 2025. Management is targeting a whopping $180 billion to $190 billion this year.

The biggest risk hasn't faded just yet, however. Investors must remain critical of and pay close attention to the long-term returns of this unprecedented level of capex. Chief Financial Officer Anat Ashkenazi said that capex will "significantly increase" in 2027.

Story Continues

On a positive note, no rational market participant will deny that this company is a driving force in the AI boom . Alphabet is essentially involved in all phases of the game, from chip development (with its Tensor Processing Units, which are seeing strong demand) and Google Cloud to its Gemini large language models, user-facing apps, and advertising tools.

It's quite remarkable that at its scale, Alphabet is growing at the pace it is. I suspect that if there's a notable slowdown, the market would likely become more pessimistic about the stock quickly. Right now, though, there's little reason to worry.

What about the valuation?

Alphabet presents a worthwhile opportunity before June 2026, given the favorable trends already discussed. However, investors should also consider the valuation .

This booming tech stock doesn't trade at an outlandish price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio. While its P/E of 29.6 is 62% higher than a year ago, the fundamental momentum will make anyone bullish.

According to the average of analyst estimates, Alphabet's diluted earnings per share are projected to increase at a compound annual rate of almost 17% between 2025 and 2028. This outlook could certainly come up short, as forecasts have generally gone up thanks to the company's impressive performance and profit gains.

Alphabet has been on an incredible run. Investors who buy the stock before June 2026 are sure to benefit.

news.Title: 台股衝破43000！三大法人買超642億 外資大買「這檔」9.2萬張
news.Content:
台股今日大漲1376點，收43644點，成交量來到1.3兆。三大法人合計買超642.88億元，連3日同步買超。

觀察籌碼動向，自營商買超68.88億元，投信買超79.41億元，外資及陸資買超494.59億元，合計買超642.88億元，外資連3日同步買超，金額達到1859.37億元；投信連6日買超，金額達到594.25億元。

以下為外資買超前十名：

主動統一升級50（00403A）買超9萬2888張，國泰永續高股息（00878）買超7萬5375張，華邦電（2344）買超7萬2092張，力積電（6770）買超7萬137張，仁寶（2324）買超5萬7815張，鴻海（2317）買超5萬2052張，聯電（2303）買超4萬8520張，台玻（1802）買超4萬3213張，群益台灣精選高股息（00919）買超3萬1703張，主動群益台灣強棒（00982A）買超3萬1703張。

（封面圖／東森新聞）

news.Title: 傳大砍員工分紅 台積電闢謠：員工分紅增幅將優於去年
news.Content:
台積電 (2330-TW)(TSM-US) 員工於臉書社團中爆料，內部擬大砍員工 10-15% 分紅，原因竟是社會觀感不佳，引發市場震撼、基層更強烈反彈，甚至揚言效法三星發動罷工行動。台積電今 (25) 日回應，今年員工分紅增幅將超過去年，更強調隨著公司未來穩健發展，大家的分紅將會持續增加，也一舉粉碎不實謠言。

台積電指出，公司清楚認知台積電在台灣有著越來越大的企業社會責任，在盈餘分配上，公司會進一步加大對社會永續資源投入的比重回饋台灣。

台積電感謝員工對於公司的貢獻，今年隨著公司成長，公司很有信心台積電員工分紅 (依績效考核) 全年成長將超過去年分紅成長，也相信伴隨公司未來的穩健發展，大家的分紅將會持續增加。

news.Title: 《日股》日經收漲2.87% 首破65000點創高
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-25 16:45:16 數位內容中心 發佈

日股今(25)日走強，並創下收盤新高。日經225指數收於65158.19點，上漲1819.12點或2.87%，連續3個交易日上漲，盤中最高觸及65408.87點；東證股價指數(TOPIX)收於3942.57點，上漲50.11點或1.29%，亦刷新歷史紀錄。指數的主要推升動能來自地緣政治緩和預期與科技題材延續，並伴隨期貨走強，整體市場呈現指數創高但盤面分化的格局。

宏觀與外部驅動方面，美國與伊朗和談進展的消息降低了地緣政治風險，使WTI原油價格下跌，市場風險偏好因此回升，成為推動股市走強的重要外部因素。同時，美股在半導體與AI題材領軍上漲的帶動下，漲勢的樂觀氣氛進一步外溢至日股，這些因素共同壓低避險資產利率並推升風險性資產價格。

個股方面，軟銀集團(9984.JP)被視為推升日經225的重要貢獻股，東京威力科創(8035.JP)與愛德萬測試(6857.JP)等半導體設備股以及鎧俠(285A.JP)、藤倉(5803.JP)在盤中及收盤表現強勢；相對地，Life Corporation(8194.JP)因目標價下調而出現大幅下跌。整體來看，資金明顯集中於少數大型權值股與AI/半導體族群。

《日股》日經收跌近1%、連三跌

《歐股》憂通膨失控、歐股挫 特諾本逆勢飆

news.Title: The SpaceX IPO Could Raise $75 Billion. History Suggests This Stock Will Be a Bigger Winner Than SpaceX Itself.
news.Content:
SpaceX is gearing up for what could be the largest initial public offering (IPO) in history, targeting a capital raise of around $75 billion and a valuation between $1.75 trillion and $2 trillion . Investors are lining up to own a piece of Elon Musk's company, which is revolutionizing rockets, satellite internet, and potentially even AI-native data centers.

While Wall Street fixates on SpaceX's debut, history offers a subtle but compelling lesson: The biggest long-term winners from hyped-up IPOs are rarely the headline companies themselves. Instead, they are usually the essential suppliers powering ambitious visions behind the scenes.

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on a little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly," providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need.

Continue »

In the case of SpaceX, the company that best fits that description is Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) . The money that the company raises with this stock sale is expected to fuel a major spending spree on advanced computing, and Nvidia is poised to capture an outsize share of it -- much like other infrastructure enablers have during past tech booms.

History shows that infrastructure providers often outshine

Stock market history is filled with examples of pick-and-shovel players that become more lucrative investments than the companies that were directly chasing the actual "gold rush."

During the internet era of the late 1990s, numerous dot-com companies skyrocketed following their IPOs on enthusiastic narratives -- only to crash once reality impinged on their optimistic visions. Meanwhile, Cisco Systems (NASDAQ: CSCO) made a mint by providing the routers and switches that made web development possible in its early days. Until the dot-com bubble burst in 2000, ongoing demand for Cisco's hardware brought sustained returns to its investors.

Similarly, during the cloud computing surge of the 2010s, data center builders like Amazon (NASDAQ: AMZN) and Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) entered the spotlight. However, it was chipmakers enabling the underlying processing power that captured the most durable upside.

The patterns are clear: IPO proceeds don't vanish into thin air. Instead, the capital these companies raise flows downstream to the vendors whose technology makes innovation feasible. The $75 billion war chest SpaceX hopes to build underscores this idea. The company is vertically integrated in many ways, yet it will remain highly dependent on other companies' cutting-edge GPU architectures for autonomous flight simulations and powering orbital data networks.

Story Continues

Will Nvidia benefit from the SpaceX IPO?

Musk has confirmed that, even as SpaceX invests in developing its own custom silicon in-house, it will continue buying Nvidia hardware at scale post-IPO.

The cash infusion from the IPO will accelerate projects such as expanding Starlink's footprint, advancing Starship reusability, and designing data centers that could eventually be placed in orbit. Each of these initiatives demands clusters of GPUs for training models and deploying real-time inference. As such, Nvidia's relationship with SpaceX remains structural and recurring (for now).

Nvidia's near-monopoly in the general-purpose AI accelerator market should result in a meaningful slice of SpaceX's newly gained liquidity flowing into its thriving data center chip business. Meanwhile, I suspect SpaceX stock will experience the typical bouts of post-IPO volatility, particularly after lock-up agreements expire and insiders begin taking profits.

Nvidia stock is positioned to compound in the long run

What truly sets Nvidia apart is the durability of its competitive advantages. SpaceX's IPO will be just another catalyst for the GPU giant. As Musk's integrated AI-space economy materializes, demand for powerful computing hardware should only intensify.

Nvidia's ecosystem of GPU architecture, its CUDA software platform, and its networking equipment makes the company a full-stack solution. This structure creates a formidable moat that competitors have struggled to breach throughout the AI revolution.

Investors who bet on the SpaceX IPO itself will end up chasing short-term momentum pops. But those who own Nvidia stock will be positioned to enjoy multiyear tailwinds driven by accelerating investment in AI infrastructure.

news.Title: 黃仁勳會張忠謀誇讚「身體好」　27日將出席輝達總部動土儀式
news.Content:
即時中心／林韋慈報導

輝達執行長黃仁勳與台積電創辦人張忠謀昨（24）晚餐敘後，接受媒體訪問時表示，張忠謀身體狀況很好，兩人聊得非常愉快，無話不談。黃仁勳進一步透露此次在台行程安排，他表示將會見多位重要合作夥伴，並希望能見到廣達董事長林百里（Barry），預計週二晚上將與台積電總裁魏哲家（CC）會面。這週他將有多場重要會議。

黃仁勳將出席27日輝達台北市科總部動土儀式

除了與供應鏈高層進行閉門餐敘，黃仁勳此行也將出席27日輝達北士科總部動土儀式，以及即將登場的台北電腦展。他強調，輝達新一代超級晶片平台「Vera Rubin」是公司史上規模最大、速度最快的產品，預計需要台灣約150家生態系夥伴共同參與打造，台灣接下來將會非常忙碌。

來台期間，黃仁勳密集與台灣AI供應鏈高層舉行閉門會議，確認下半年AI晶片出貨時程、產能配置以及新世代產品開發方向。專家指出，台灣供應鏈在晶圓代工、先進封裝等關鍵領域具備完整優勢，將在全球AI市場扮演核心角色。

中經院科技政策評估研究中心副主任戴志言分析表示，台灣從晶片、伺服器、算力中心解決方案，一直到個人裝置端都能完整提供，「基本上通通能包辦」，且能在三到四小時的聚落鏈結內完成，顯示台灣在AI供應鏈上的高度整合能力。

原文出處： 快新聞／黃仁勳會張忠謀誇讚「身體好」 27日將出席輝達總部動土儀式

news.Title: 台積電今年員工依績效考核分紅，全年分紅成長比例將勝去年
news.Content: 【財訊快報／記者李純君報導】三星的罷工事件尚未平息，上週臉書社團「TSMC大小事」爆出，有部分員工因不滿傳言台積電(2330)可能會砍15%分紅，釋出不排除效法三星員工進行罷工，台積電今日則公開針對分紅，提出官方說明，強調全年成長比例將超過去年分紅成長。台積電表示，我們清楚認知台積電在台灣有著越來越大的企業社會責任，在盈餘分配上，我們會進一步加大對社會永續資源投入的比重回饋台灣，尤其台積電感謝員工對於公司的貢獻，今年隨著公司成長，我們很有信心台積電員工分紅（依績效考核）全年成長比例將超過去年分紅成長，也相信，伴隨公司未來的穩健發展，大家的分紅將會持續增加。

news.Title: 有錢買不到！聯發科秒鎖漲停噴天價 背後原因震撼市場
news.Content:
台股今（25）日再度上演驚人漲勢，收盤大漲1376點、創第5大漲點，指數站穩4萬3千點續創新高。其中，IC設計龍頭聯發科成為盤面最強焦點，股價一開盤即跳空漲停鎖死，勁揚385元至4245元，強勢突破4000元整數關卡，更同步刷新歷史新高紀錄。

聯發科此波強攻的最大推手，來自外資里昂證券最新發布的研究報告。里昂大幅調升聯發科目標價，從原本4500元一口氣上修至5922元，調幅高達31%，並重申「優於大盤」評等，點燃投資人對AI題材的高度期待。

根據里昂報告，Google最新TPU（張量處理器）發展藍圖出現重大變化，原本由博通主導、僅啟動1個月的Pumafish（TPU v9）專案，近期已遭取消，改由新專案Whalefish接棒。在此調整之下，聯發科的重要性明顯提升，市場認為其角色已從過去的「替代供應商」，正式升級為Google AI晶片戰略中的「核心夥伴」。

里昂進一步指出，聯發科旗下AI ASIC（特殊應用積體電路）業務未來成長潛力驚人。隨著與Google合作持續深化，預估到2028年，相關專案可望替聯發科帶來高達360億美元營收，占整體營收比重甚至有機會衝上六成，AI商機被視為聯發科未來數年最重要的成長引擎。

外資高盛先前率先拋出聯發科5000元的目標價，帶動股價強漲，還因漲勢太猛遭列處置股，後於21日出關後持續強攻，恢復正常交易後買氣大爆發。

除了AI布局獲得市場高度關注，聯發科近期也傳出成為國際晶圓代工大廠積極爭取的關鍵客戶。市場消息指出，三星會長李在鎔日前低調訪台，並親自拜會聯發科執行長蔡力行。外界推測，三星有意爭取聯發科部分晶圓代工訂單，希望與台積電正面競爭，也凸顯聯發科在全球AI供應鏈中的戰略地位持續攀升。

news.Title: 黃仁勳蘇姿丰陳立武齊聚台北！　三大AI巨頭助台股攻頂
news.Content:
【緯來新聞網】台北國際電腦展（COMPUTEX 2026）即將在6月2日登場，輝達執行長黃仁勳、超微董事長蘇姿丰與英特爾執行長陳立武三大華人AI巨頭相繼抵台，三家公司市值合計近6.5兆美元。

（圖／路透社提供）

黃仁勳已於23日抵台，並將於6月1日進行主題演講；蘇姿丰則於5月中旬提前來台，宣布將投資超過100億美元深化台灣產業鏈合作。陳立武也將在展覽期間發表關鍵策略演說，力拚透過Panther Lake處理器、異質運算及先進製程強化英特爾在台生態系的布局。

台股今（25）日早盤大漲超過1200點，盤中最高觸及43564點，續創歷史新高。

news.Title: 美元失守99＋台股飛越4萬3，亞幣強勢突圍，台幣週一急升近1角
news.Content:
【財訊快報／陳孟朔】中東和平進程露出曙光，油價急跌壓抑美元避險買盤，美元指數週一(25日)失守99關口，亞洲貨幣普遍獲得升值支撐。新台幣兌美元匯率在台股強勢破頂與區域資金風險偏好回升助攻下，週一收盤急升9.8分，以31.470元作收，盤中交投介於31.394元至31.520元之間，成交量放大至18億8,300萬美元，顯示外匯市場買盤明顯轉向新台幣。新台幣升勢的第一道推力來自美元轉弱。市場押注美國與伊朗若能推進和平框架，荷姆茲海峽最終有機會恢復通航，能源供應中斷與全球通膨惡化風險隨之降溫，國際油價亞洲電子盤中一度大跌近6%。在能源通膨與美國利率維持高檔的壓力同步緩解下，美元失去近期最重要的避險與高息支撐，人民幣早盤升破6.79元關卡、改寫近40個月高位，也進一步為包括新台幣在內的亞洲貨幣營造升值氣氛。

台股創高則成為新台幣第二道強力推進器。台灣加權股價指數在前兩個交易日累計大漲5.55%後，週一再度狂飆1,376.43點或3.26%，收在43,644.40點歷史新高，首次站上43,000點大關，並在韓股休市之際成為亞洲股市表現最強市場。市場人士表示，台股由AI與半導體權值股帶動的攻頂行情，吸引資金重新追逐台灣科技資產，匯市結匯需求與資金回流預期同步升高，使新台幣仍能守住大部分升值成果至收盤。

news.Title: 《台股盤後》開高走高、飆漲1376點，首收43K
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-25 16:02:55 新聞中心 發佈

美股四大指數上週五全面收紅、美伊可望停戰又激勵原油期貨電子盤明顯走弱，加上輝達/AMD執行長紛紛來台、帶動市場對COMPUTEX題材的樂觀預期，台股今(25)日由台積電、聯發科、日月光投控、台達電等電子權值股領軍強攻，搭配國巨*領軍的被動元件、群創/友達領軍的光電股、昇達科領軍的衛星概念股續強，維繋住市場人氣，雖19大類股漲跌幾乎各半、長榮集團遭壓盤/台塑四寶僅南亞走揚，但鋼鐵股強勢上漲、國泰金/富邦金強拉尾盤，挹注加權指數終場收在43644.40點、日K連三紅，大漲1376.43點或3.26%，刷新收盤新高，成交量擴大至1兆3013.25億元。

結束中東衝突談判似有進展、激勵道瓊工業平均指數躍上收盤新高，輔以高通等半導體股跳漲，也讓費城半導體指數亦創下收盤新高紀錄。亞洲股市方面，因費半指數上週五創高，激勵日本相關股今日飆漲，加上權重股軟銀集團(SBG)走揚，帶動日經225指數首度飆破6萬5千點整數大關，收漲2.87%；韓股因適逢佛誕節補假，休市一天。

台股今日走勢呈現開高走高，電子權值股漲勢凌厲，台積電重返2300元，終場收在最高點2310元、上漲2.44%，聯發科、日月光投控、台光電、聯電亮燈漲停，台達電、致茂、金像電、鴻海、創意同步走強。被動元件族群受惠AI浪潮、高階被動元件短缺，成今日盤面焦點，國巨*、日電貿、千如、勤凱、天二科技、華新科、信昌電等多檔收在漲停板價位。光電類股亦持續走升，群創、友達、彩晶、一詮、瑞軒、乙盛-KY齊鎖漲停。

在29大類股表現上，由電子零組件類股領漲、收漲6.23%；鋼鐵類股受惠基本面改善、訂單動能回溫，收漲5.96%；光電股在下游需求回穩預期帶動下亦有好表現，上漲5.75%。而在弱勢股部分，以貿易百貨、油電燃氣類股跌勢相對較深，跌幅均超過2%。

三大法人動向方面，今日三大法人合計買超642.88億元；其中，自營商買超68.88億元，投信買超79.42億元，外資及陸資買超494.59億元。

《台股盤後》飆漲1347點、重返41K，寫第五大漲點

《台股盤後》下跌154點、連四黑K，險守4萬大關

資料來源- MoneyDJ理財網

news.Title: 台股站穩4萬3寫收盤新高！半導體權值股領軍　0050回歸百元俱樂部
news.Content:
受惠於人工智慧（AI）熱潮與地緣政治風險降溫，台股今（25）日表現氣勢如虹，加權指數終場大漲1376.43點，以43644.40點作收，寫下史上第五大單日收盤漲點，並創下歷史收盤新高。在熱錢持續湧入下，集中市場也爆出1.3兆的成交量。

市場傳出美伊停火協議接近完成，國際油價應聲重挫逾5%，有效緩解了市場對中東局勢的擔憂情緒。此外，適逢台北國際電腦展（COMPUTEX）即將登場，超微（AMD）執行長蘇姿丰、輝達（NVIDIA）執行長黃仁勳接連訪台，進一步引爆AI概念股的強勁走勢。

權值股全面噴發 聯發科、台積電領軍衝鋒

電子權值股今日成為盤面主攻焦點，「護國神山」台積電（2330）開高走高，終場收在今日最高價2310元，上漲55元，單一個股便挹注大盤約455點。

聯發科（2454）表現更為強勢，開盤便直攻漲停價4245元，創下歷史新天價。其他半導體權值股如聯電（2303）、日月光投控（3711）也雙雙攻上漲停板，終場分別收在125元與617元。鴻海則上漲11元，收在261元，整體電子族群呈現百花齊放的熱絡景象。

AI伺服器商機發酵，台達電強勢卡位

電源供應器龍頭台達電（2308）受惠於AI基礎建設升級商機，今日股價跳空開高，終場大漲195元，收在2290元，漲幅高達9.31%。台達電目前在AI伺服器電源解決方案的市占率已超過50%至60%，並積極推進高壓直流（HVDC）與液冷方案。

法人指出，隨著AI伺服器出貨放量，台達電今年第一季AI相關營收占比已達約40%，成功推升毛利率至37%的歷史新高。預期至今年底，其AI相關產品營收占比有望挑戰50%，全年合併毛利率表現有機會逐季增溫。

ETF市場熱絡 0050強勢重返百元俱樂部

伴隨大盤不斷創高，市值型ETF龍頭元大台灣50（0050）今日盤中最高來到101元，終場收在100.8元，強勢奪回百元大關。0050自去年6月完成「1拆4」股票分割後，入手門檻大幅降低為5萬元有找，不到一年便吸引超過172萬名新股民進場，受益人數直逼300萬大關。

若投資人從去年拆股後降為47.16元買進並持有至今，每張股票帳面獲利已超過5萬元，漲幅呈現翻倍。

此外，主動式ETF如統一台股增長（00981A）也創下31.75元的新高價，而主動統一升級50（00403A）則再奪人氣王，以71.4萬張成交量居冠，顯示市場資金在市值型與主動型ETF族群間皆呈現積極輪動的態勢。

news.Title: Walmart vs. Costco: Which Is the Better "Recession-Proof" Stock to Buy Now?
news.Content:
The major stock market indexes are hovering around all-time highs, but the economy is chock-full of cracks beneath the surface.

Higher gas prices are adding even more pressure on already strained consumers. The housing market is in a prolonged, multi-year slowdown. Wage growth isn't keeping up with the cost of living, and the labor market faces profound paradigm shifts driven by artificial intelligence (AI).

Will AI create the world's first trillionaire? Our team just released a report on the one little-known company, called an "Indispensable Monopoly" providing the critical technology Nvidia and Intel both need. Continue »

Here's why Walmart (NASDAQ: WMT) and Costco Wholesale (NASDAQ: COST) are two of the most "recession-proof" stocks out there, why they may appeal to risk-averse investors, and which is the better buy now.

Value is in vogue

Walmart and Costco have operating margins of just 4.2% and 3.8%, respectively -- meaning they are converting around just $0.04 of every dollar in sales into operating income. But both companies offset these low margins with massive sales volume, passing along value to customers by offering low prices through elite supply chains and efficient operations.

Walmart and Costco continue to control a greater portion of their shelf space through their private labels: Costco Wholesale's Kirkland Signature, Walmart's Great Value, and Sam's Club's Member's Mark. While name brands have to justify higher prices through quality guarantees and customer trust, private labels undercut them and deliver more value, which is exactly what folks looking to stretch their dollars are seeking.

Walmart and Costco have expanded far beyond their in-store experiences, with Walmart rapidly gaining ground in e-commerce through its Walmart+ home delivery subscription service. Walmart and Costco both offer tons of services, from fuel and auto care to pharmacy, health and wellness, and financial services. Since value is ingrained in both companies' brand messaging, Walmart and Costco have grown their top and bottom lines steadily, while many other retailers and consumer goods companies are experiencing revenue and margin declines.

Priced for perfection

Walmart and Costco are two of the most recession-proof businesses. The problem is that the market already recognizes their competitive advantages and has awarded both stocks with sky-high valuations. Walmart trades at 44.4 times trailing earnings and 41.5 times forward earnings, while Costco is even more expensive at 54.6 times trailing earnings and 51.1 times forward earnings.

Story Continues

Despite their slow-to-moderate growth rates, investors have gravitated toward both stocks in recent years. In fact, Walmart and Costco have nearly tripled in the last five years -- outperforming the red-hot tech sector, Nasdaq Composite , and S&P 500 while the consumer staples sector as a whole has broadly underperformed the major indexes.

COST Total Return Level data by YCharts.

There are far better buys than Walmart or Costco

Given their nose-bleed valuations, most investors are probably better off not buying either safe stock right now. But if I had to choose one, I'd go with Walmart.

The company is leveraging AI across its operations and customer experience, and there's every reason to believe Walmart can continue to become a better-run company by adopting new AI tools. Walmart also has a better dividend than Costco, with 53 consecutive years of increasing its payout.

While Costco yields just 0.6% compared to Walmart's 0.8%, Costco does occasionally (usually every three years or so) pay special dividends when it deems it has enough cash on its balance sheet to distribute to investors. In general, I'm not a fan of special dividends, because they defeat the purpose of generating scheduled passive income. At least with Walmart, I can count on the dividend gradually growing with time.

Walmart and Costco are two impeccable companies, but they are priced even more expensively than top-tier growth stocks like Nvidia , Broadcom , Alphabet , Meta Platforms , Amazon , Apple , and Microsoft . I'd take any of these names over Walmart and Costco in May.

news.Title: 台積、聯發科帶動AI股漲勢 台股收盤4萬3644點
news.Content:
美股上週五四大指數收紅，以及美伊戰事可望達成協議，油價回落，週一亞股跟著狂歡，日股收盤漲2.87%；台股更狂，收盤站穩4萬3千點，以43644點作收、大漲1376點，創史上第5大漲點。領漲高價股，像是台積電收在最高2310元、漲55元，開盤秒奔漲停鎖死的聯發科，終場以4245元作收、漲385元。

證券投顧總經理陳柏州指出，「領軍的當然就是老AI跟台積電的一個相關供應鏈設備，或者是產物為主，那我們也可以看到，資金來講其實也是呈現一個明顯的擴散，可以看得出是往像一些被動元件或是矽晶圓族群。」

在AI族群群起上攻下，台股成功達陣4萬3千點，成交值達到新台幣1兆3013.25億元，三大法人合計買超台股642.88億元。統計5月以來到現在，台股已狂漲4600多點，總共有51檔千金股合體。

金馬年不只台股價量齊揚，經濟表現更是不俗，台經院表示，今（2026）年台灣經濟，除了AI帶來訂單，傳產也有起色，如果下半年中東戰事停戰、油價平穩，加上AI需求暢旺，台灣經濟成長率，可望再上修。

台經院景氣預測中心孫明德表示，「台灣經濟成長率，我們台經院上次的預估大概是7%多，譬如說中東戰爭已經結束了，我們的傳產外銷出口，能夠持續這樣的榮景，當這些因素都齊備的時候，我想台灣經濟成長率，當然還有再上修的空間。」

儘管股市看似呈現多頭，但也有專家提醒，市場上出現券商沒錢可借、個人房貸餘額還有個人信貸餘額，都創新紀錄，市場正上演結構性資金荒，萬一行情反轉，可能造成去槓桿賣壓出籠，對台股形成修正。

news.Title: 台積、聯發科帶動AI股漲勢 台股早盤一度衝破4萬3千點
news.Content:
美股上週五四大指數收紅，加上美伊戰事可望達成協議，油價回落，週一亞股跟著一起狂歡，日股早盤大漲超過3%，台股也不落人後，在輝達執行長 黃仁勳 提前抵台為台北國際電腦展暖身，台灣相關AI供應鏈股價跟著飆出新高價，台積電早盤最高一度2310元、漲55元，聯發科秒奔漲停鎖死，大漲385元來到4245元。

證券投顧總經理陳柏州指出，「領軍的當然就是老AI跟台積電的一個相關供應鏈設備，或者是產物為主那我們也可以看到，資金來講其實也是呈現一個明顯的擴散，可以看得出是往像一些被動元件或是矽晶圓族群。」

在AI族群群起上攻下，台股早盤一度達陣4萬3千點，最高4萬3628點、漲超過1300點。統計5月以來到現在，台股已狂漲4600多點，總共有51檔千金股合體。

金馬年一路奔騰的台股呈現價量齊揚，但市場上也出現券商沒錢可借、個人房貸餘額還有個人信貸餘額，都創新紀錄，專家提醒市場正上演結構性資金荒，萬一行情反轉，可能造成去槓桿賣壓出籠，對台股形成修正。提前抵台的黃仁勳，週一預計拜訪廣達董事長林百里，週二和台積電董事長魏哲家餐敘，週三參加輝達台灣總部舉辦的會員大會。

輝達執行長黃仁勳說：「我想我們週三會舉行新大樓的動土典禮，市長可能會出席，也許還有其他人也會到場，我很期待見到他們。」

黃仁勳旋風式來台，專家分析，如果中東情勢沒出現惡化，隨著台北國際電腦展即將登場，台股氛圍仍偏向樂觀，可望延續多頭發展。

news.Title: 【Follow法人】外資狂買494億「還留一手」！掃貨2檔記憶體股分別逾7萬張 加碼鴻海5萬張
news.Content:
（下午4時49分更新外資買賣超個股）

三大法人今（25）日齊點火，買超642.88億元，其中，外資大買494.59億元。對此，分析師表示，今日外資買盤部分受美股休市影響而未完全釋放，加上新台幣持續升值，預期明日有望迎來外資「補買潮」。觀察買超個股，外資資金集中火力布局電子股，買超華邦電（2344）、力積電（6770）分別逾7萬張，並且連3買鴻海（2317），今再加碼逾5萬張。

外資今日買超前三大：00403A、00878、華邦電

主動式ETF續熱，外資大掃主動統一升級50（00403A）9萬2,888張、主動群益台灣強棒（00982A）3萬1,703張；另同步加碼國泰永續高股息（00878）7萬5,375張，呈連4買，合計買超逾16萬張。

此外，外資大舉買進記憶體股華邦電（2344）、力積電（6770）分別為7萬2,092張、7萬137張；老AI股仁寶（2324）、鴻海（2317）也大獲外資青睞，分別掃貨逾5萬張。

外資今日賣超前三大：台新新光金、第一金、華南金

台股表現火熱，外資調節前三名皆為金融股，包括台新新光金（2887）5萬1,063張、第一金（2892）4萬6,851張以及華南金（2880）2萬6,168張。除此之外，賣超廣達（2382）1萬9,827張，終結連2買。

根據證交所公布籌碼動向，三大法人今日合計買超642.88億元，其中外資買超494.59億元，累計3個交易日買超高達1859.37億元。

投信買超79.42億元，連續6日買超台股達594.25億元。

自營商方面，今買超68.88億元，其中自行買賣部分買超44.75億元，避險方面買超24.12億元。

啟發投顧分析師白易弘受訪表示，台股目前處於主升段，而外資今日買超力道沒有上周五多，主要是美國今為陣亡將士紀念日（Memorial Day）休市「若沒有休市，會買更多」；再加上今日新台幣升值逾一角，預期外資明天會有一波「補買潮」。

針對市場擔憂過熱問題，白易弘則認為，目前仍未看到明顯過熱跡象，主因AI與半導體產業趨勢持續向上，包括先進製程、CPU等領域未來展望與需求預期都持續上修；台股目前主要漲勢動能來自投信與ETF買盤推升，整體資金面仍偏正向。

受美伊停戰協議有望達成激勵，市場避險情緒明顯降溫，帶動台股今日跳空大漲，強勢突破4萬3千點整數關卡，在權值股、AI供應鏈等族群全面點火下，終場收在43,644.4點，勁揚1376.43點、刷新歷史新高紀錄。

王詩婷／財經特派記者，主跑勞動部、半導體、生技等

news.Title: AI狂潮帶飛台股創新天價 三大法人聯手買超642.88億元
news.Content:
美伊戰爭有望達成協議、黃仁勳提前來台，台股今 (25) 日 AI 狂潮噴出，收盤上漲 1376 點或 3.26%，漲點創歷史第五大，收 43644.4 點創新高，多方搶買積極，日 K 線跳空缺口達 310 點，成交量 1.3 兆元；三大法人合計買超 642.88 億元。

觀察三大法人今天籌碼動向，外資買超 494.59 億；自營商賣買超 68.88 億元；投信買超 79.42 億元；三大法人合計買超 642.88 億元。

news.Title: 韓媒：美光科技HBM4產能倍增 2027年量產轉由台積電代工的HBM4E
news.Content:
韓媒《THE ELEC》周一 (25 日) 報導，美光科技( MU-US )第六代高頻寬記憶體 (HBM4) 產能爬坡進展順利，速度較去年 HBM3 12 層產品提升兩倍，良率同步改善。

美光全球營運副總裁 Manish Bhatia 在摩根大通會議上透露，HBM4 將用於輝達下一代 Rubin AI 運算平台，成為關鍵供應商。

美光表示，HBM4 效能與穩定性顯著提升，得益於 HBM3/HBM3E 的量產經驗，其核心 DRAM 採用成熟的 1β(10 奈米 5 代) 製程，搭配自研基底晶片。

在更先進的 HBM4E 世代，美光將進行戰略調整，核心 DRAM 升級至首次導入 EUV 的 1γ(10 奈米 6 代) 製程，基底晶片則不再自研，轉由台積電代工。美光打算在 2027 年量產，首批將推出 JEDEC 標準品，並同步開發客製化版本。

相較於美國競爭對手，三星電子預計今年第二季送出 HBM4E 樣品，基底仍維持自製，SK 海力士則打算 2026 年下半年送樣、2027 年量產，基底採用台積電 3 奈米製程。

美光同時設定目標，2026 年年中 1γ DRAM 與第 9 代 NAND 出貨量將佔總容量一半以上，使 1γ成為該公司最大單一 DRAM 製程。

業界人士分析，隨著 AI 算力需求激增，HBM 技術競賽進入白熱化，美光透過與台積電合作，有望在高效能與良率控制上縮小與韓系大廠差距，爭奪 AI 伺服器記憶體市場主導權。

news.Title: 追擊台積電、不靠ASML？華為喊技術突破 2031年量產1.4奈米
news.Content:
華為技術（Huawei Technologies）表示，已提出一條新的技術路徑，有望縮小與產業龍頭台積電 ( 2330-TW ) ( TSM-US ) 之間的差距，甚至在缺乏最先進設備的情況下，實現先進半導體製造的突破。目前，台積電的製程能力，約領先華為及其代工夥伴中芯國際約 5 年。

華為半導體業務負責人何庭波於週一 (25 日) 指出，華為將憑藉自主研發的「LogicFolding」（邏輯折疊）技術，將於 2031 年前開始生產 1.4 奈米晶片；相較之下，台積電先前已宣布，於 2028 年啟動該製程晶片的量產。

若華為能成功實現 1.4 奈米半導體的大規模量產，這將打破業界的普遍共識，即必須使用荷蘭大廠艾司摩爾（ASML）最先進的極紫外光（EUV）微影設備，才能量產 5 奈米或更先進的晶片。此類半導體主要用於驅動當前最尖端的人工智慧（AI）技術。

奈米通常用來衡量晶片上電晶體的尺寸。電晶體愈微縮，單一晶片上能容納的數量就愈多，進而提升晶片的運算效能。而 ASML 的 EUV 設備，正是被視為電晶體持續微縮不可或缺的關鍵利器。

何庭波指出，2025 年推出麒麟 9030Pro 後，華為手機晶片進入效能「飽和區」。為此，華為基於以「時間縮微」取代「幾何縮微」的新定律，找到新的路徑，使手機晶片性能實現階躍式提升。

根據何庭波介紹，「麒麟 2026」手機晶片是邏輯摺疊技術的首次成功實施。它基於全新的自由邏輯設計理念，由單層擴展至了雙層，並實現晶體管密度等指標的大幅提升。

近年來摩爾定律面臨物理極限和經濟效益雙重挑戰。隨著晶體管「幾何縮微」放緩，成本紅利逐漸消退，如何跨越傳統工藝路徑的侷限，探索出一條全新可持續演進路線，以滿足當下呈指數級攀升的運算性能需求，已成為全球半導體行業亟待攻克的共同難題。

總部位於深圳的華為，在美國主導、多國參與的多年先進晶片與設備出口限制攻勢下，已然成為北京推動半導體自主化的急先鋒。這些制裁在一定程度上制約了中國在人工智慧領域的發展步伐。

華為於 9 月公布了一項為期三年的路線圖，計劃推出一系列 AI 晶片，以填補美方禁止晶片巨頭輝達 ( NVDA-US ) 向中國出口最先進半導體後所留下的市場真空。

news.Content:

過去幾年，台灣投資人對 ETF 的熱愛，幾乎全被高股息與市值型給包辦了。但在台股衝上高點、AI 與半導體供應鏈進入超級週期的推動下，產業輪動速度快到讓人目不暇給。傳統被動追蹤指數的 ETF，因為具有調整落 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 在AI浪潮及產業輪動加速下，台股主動式ETF爆紅，吸引千億資金進駐。
• 主動式ETF由專業經理人操盤，可隨市場風向即時調整策略，追求超額報酬。

news.Title: Billionaire Bill Gates' Foundation Sold Its Microsoft Stake and Has 43% of Its $33 Billion Portfolio Invested in 2 Other Stocks
news.Content: Bill Gates is best known as the co-founder of Microsoft (NASDAQ: MSFT) , which has been one of the most valuable companies in the world for the past 30 years. After his wealth soared to more than $100 billion in the 1990s amid the run-up in tech stocks , Gates started to step back from his business to focus on his philanthropic endeavors. He founded the Gates Foundation to combat poverty, disease, and inequity worldwide. Today, Gates is still worth over $100 billion, despite donating huge sums to the foundation, including a considerable number of Microsoft shares. He ...
news.AI_Summary:
• The Bill Gates Foundation has sold its remaining Microsoft stake, reducing its direct exposure.
• The Foundation's $33 billion portfolio is now heavily invested in value stocks, with Berkshire Hathaway being the top holding (approx. 25%).
• Despite the sale from the trust, Bill Gates personally owns 103 million shares of Microsoft, valued at approximately $43 billion.
• Microsoft's cloud computing and software businesses are experiencing rapid growth, driven by AI demand, and trade at an attractive forward P/E of 25x.
• The article highlights Berkshire Hathaway's solid Q1 results under new CEO Greg Abel, with improvements in insurance underwriting and railroad operations.
news.Keywords: philanthropy, portfolio allocation, value investing, cloud growth, forward P/E

news.Title: 群創挑戰50元大關！面板股東會本週登場 　市場關注焦點一次看
news.Content: 【緯來新聞網】台股今（25）日延續強勢多頭行情，加權指數早盤以42667.87點開出後，電子族群全面點火，其中面板三雄同步爆發。群創、友達與彩晶盤中全數亮燈漲停，吸引大量資金追價，成 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 面板三雄群創、友達、彩晶今日同步亮燈漲停，其中群創股價逼近50元大關，創2010年4月以來新高。
• 此波面板股上漲歸因於AI供應鏈、先進封裝與高階應用等新題材，而非僅傳統景氣循環。
• 市場關注本週即將召開的面板雙虎股東會，預計將聚焦AI先進封裝FOPLP、車用智慧移動及矽光子等新事業布局。
• 群創FOPLP已進入量產階段，並透過Chip First、RDL等技術布局行動裝置、車用電子及AI伺服器市場，預計2026年貢獻度有望放大。
• 市場傳聞台積電可能攜手群創發展面板級封裝技術，進一步推升資金關注。
news.Keywords: 面板股, AI供應鏈, 先進封裝, FOPLP, 股東會

news.Title: This $8 Stock Could Be Your Ticket to Millionaire Status
news.Content: SoundHound AI (NASDAQ: SOUN) has fallen out of favor once again, but don't assume this means Wall Street has finally had it with this early-stage artificial intelligence (AI) play. The company, which focuses on developing AI-based ...
news.AI_Summary:
• SoundHound AI (SOUN), an AI-based voice assistant platform developer, is trading around $8 per share.
• The company has a diversified customer base including Stellantis and Chipotle Mexican Grill and is expanding into agentic AI.
• Revenue increased by 52% last quarter, but non-GAAP EBITDA losses have remained steady at around $25 million per quarter.

news.Keywords: voice assistant, AI startup, revenue growth, equity offering, acquisition synergies

news.Title: 台積電驚爆「砍15%分紅」！ 工程師怒了：不排除效法三星大罷工
news.Content: 有「護國神山」之稱的台灣積體電路製造公司近期驚傳擬削減員工分紅高達15%，消息一出立即在科技圈掀起震撼。由於台積電近年受惠A ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 台灣積體電路製造公司（台積電）近期傳出擬削減員工分紅高達15%，引發科技圈震撼與員工不滿。
• 員工抱怨公司高層「想改就改、毫無誠信」，並揚言效法三星發動罷工，爭取權益。
• 員工不滿在高壓工作環境下犧牲生活健康，卻面臨獎金縮減，認為無法接受。
• 外媒分析指出，台積電龐大的資本支出計畫可能影響成本控制，但若爆發大規模罷工，恐衝擊全球AI供應鏈。
• 目前分紅消息仍屬員工間流傳，台積電尚未正式證實，但員工不滿情緒持續升溫。
news.Keywords: 員工分紅, 罷工風險, 勞資爭議, AI供應鏈, 資本支出

news.Title: 台股狂漲1,300點！聯發科封神、欣興破千元「不要只追一線大廠」主動式ETF默默偏愛6檔中大型電子

news.AI_Summary:
• 受惠於美股標普500指數連八周收紅及輝達執行長黃仁勳來台掀起AI旋風，台股今日開高走高，盤中大漲逾1300點，衝破43,000點大關，續創歷史新高。
• 台積電、聯發科、鴻海等核心權值股扮演撐盤要角，聯發科開盤鎖漲停，市值衝破6.8兆元穩居台股第二大權值王。
• AI供應鏈全面爆發，從晶圓代工、晶片設計、散熱到ABF載板，欣興首度衝破千元大關，帶動台股千金股擴編至51檔。

• 市場資金卡位意願高，預期台北國際電腦展將延續暖場行情，並有企業獲利上修數據支撐。

news.Title: 台股飆新高還可以買什麼？分析師曝6月漲價概念股有這些…
news.Content: 台股不斷創新高，想買股有點擔心，但看著股市天天漲，又不想錯失賺錢的機會，處在目前的位階，想進場到底該怎麼選？證券分析師坦言，除了記憶體、被動元件、ABF、PCB這些市場已知的 ...

news.Title: 台股最大半導體ETF強勢填息 成分股近九成是「積友友」
news.Content: 美伊戰爭有望終結，油價回檔，亞股再受激勵，台股今（25）日直接突破43,000點大關，半導體族群在AI題材帶動下持續吸金，台股首檔規模突破600億元的半導體ETF中信關鍵半導體（00891）日前配息創紀錄，今也強勢完成填息 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 中信關鍵半導體ETF（00891）強勢完成填息並創掛牌新高，顯示資金持續湧入AI供應鏈。
• 00891成分股近九成與台積電供應鏈高度相關，涵蓋ASIC、先進封裝及光互連等AI關鍵領域。
• 台積電在先進製程與先進封裝的領先地位，帶動供應鏈價值提升，為半導體族群提供重要動能。
• ETF經理人指出，AI產業競爭已從製程轉向系統級整合，供應鏈價值同步提升，00891配置優勢明顯。
• 法人預期，在AI算力需求與台積電技術領先雙重支撐下，半導體族群仍是台股中長線主流。
news.Keywords: AI供應鏈,半導體ETF,台積電,先進製程,系統級整合

news.Title: Apple vs. Microsoft: What Their Revenue Trends Reveal for Investors
news.Content: Apple: Massive Scale With Seasonal Spikes Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) designs and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, and wearables worldwide, and it operates various platforms like the App Store alongside subscription-based services such as Apple TV+ and Apple Music. It recently announced an expansion of its specialized data centers and introduced lower-priced smartphone models. The company ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Apple reported $111.2 billion in sales for fiscal Q2 2026 (ended March 2026), a 17% year-over-year increase, with its services segment reaching an all-time high of $31 billion.
• Microsoft reported $82.9 billion in sales for fiscal Q3 2026 (ended March 2026), an 18% year-over-year increase, with its AI business exceeding an annual revenue run rate of $37 billion (up 123% YoY).
• Microsoft's capital expenditures increased by 49% YoY to $31.9 billion, supporting its AI expansion.
• Apple's revenue trends show significant seasonal swings, with Q4 being its largest sales period.
• Microsoft demonstrates steady quarter-over-quarter revenue growth, driven by AI tailwinds, while Apple capitalizes on product and service appeal.
news.Keywords: revenue trends, seasonal swings, AI tailwind, capital expenditures, services segment

news.Title: 看準台積熊本科技紅利，日本三井不動產熊本科學園區開發案大舉來台招商
news.Content: 【財訊快報／記者張家瑋報導】台積電(2330)全球版圖擴張，日本熊本廠已成為帶動半導體聚落成型的關鍵引擎，隨著供應鏈廠商加速進駐，不僅推升了當地工業基礎建設需求，更進一步點燃了周邊房地產與商用設施的開發商機。看準此波半導 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 日本三井不動產啟動熊本科學園區開發案，來台招商，鎖定台積電熊本廠周邊供應鏈。
• 該科學園區緊鄰台積電、索尼及東京威力科創等半導體廠，形成強大產業鏈磁吸效應。
• 開發案定位為熊本縣「分散型科學園區」核心據點，將引進半導體企業與學術機構，建構產官學合作平台。
• 崇越科技已在熊本設立營業所，提供材料、物流及技術服務，營收呈現爆發性成長。
• 法人預期，熊本科學園區開發將進一步強化產業聚落，帶動當地房地產與商業設施需求，具龐大升值潛力。
news.Keywords: 半導體聚落,供應鏈,產業園區開發,招商引資,房地產潛力

news.Title: 美光擴產恐衝擊台廠？南亞科走弱力積電漲逾5% 分析師揭兩樣情關鍵
news.Content: 美光將重返DDR4市場，加上三星電子罷工暫緩，使原先市場期待的轉單效應落空，記憶體族群今（25）日早盤股價表現 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美光宣布重返DDR4市場並擴產，加上三星罷工暫緩，導致市場期待的轉單效應落空。
• 記憶體族群今日早盤股價表現相對疲弱，南亞科下跌逾3%。
• 分析師指出，美光擴產消息短線造成利空，但對華邦電、旺宏影響有限，因其在MLC市場市占率高。
• 力積電因與美光合作關係密切，反而受惠於美光擴產消息，股價上漲。
• 南亞科因產品線與DDR4/DDR5關聯度高，且近期漲幅已大，目前處於漲多回檔階段，建議觀察。
news.Keywords: 記憶體市場,DDR4,擴產,轉單效應,個股分析

news.Title: 美光突襲！DDR4擴產嚇崩南亞科 老手曝記憶體逃命、抄底時機
news.Content: 台股今（25）日盤中大漲逾1300點，但記憶體股走勢分歧，南亞科一早下挫逾5%，主因傳出利空消息，美光宣布將大幅提升維吉尼亞州廠DDR ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美光宣布將大幅提升DDR4產能達4倍，對南亞科股價造成壓力，早盤下挫逾5%。
• 專家認為，美光擴產以長生命週期的DDR4為主，對以消費型記憶體為主的南亞科影響有限。
• 即使美光擴產，仍可能無法滿足整體市場需求，記憶體大趨勢向上，投資人無需過度恐慌。
• 從技術面看，南亞科股價接近前高套牢賣壓區，短線回檔合理，主力可能配合消息面壓低股價。
• 專家建議，短期避開波動較大的DRAM族群，中期可關注籌碼換手後的買點，等待乖離收斂後再進場。
news.Keywords: DRAM,記憶體股,美光擴產,技術分析,投資策略

news.Content: 輝達（NVIDIA）執行長黃仁勳提前抵台後行程滿檔，繼昨（24）日與台積電創辦人張忠謀夫婦共進晚餐後，外界持續關注 ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Title: 3 AI Stocks I Wouldn't Hesitate to Buy Now
news.Content: The market may have had a strong run over the past two months, but there are still plenty of stocks that look like great buys right now. The reality is that the artificial intelligence (AI) build-out is just getting started, and many of these companies have yet to recognize the full effect that an AI-first economy would have on their financials. Three AI stocks ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia (NVDA) is highlighted for its strong AI leadership, posting 85% YoY revenue growth to $81.6 billion and expecting $91 billion for Q2.
• Meta Platforms (META) shows Q1 revenue growth of 33% YoY, driven by AI improvements in its advertising business, and is considered a value play at less than 19x forward earnings.
• Amazon (AMZN) is positioned as an AI play through its cloud computing business, AWS, which saw revenue accelerate to 28% YoY, its best growth in nearly four years.
• Nvidia anticipates data center expenditures from AI hyperscalers to surpass $1 trillion in 2027, presenting a significant growth opportunity.
• Amazon is investing $200 billion in data center capital expenditures this year, expecting major growth acceleration as this capacity comes online.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, revenue acceleration, data center spending, cloud computing, value play

news.Content:

美光擴產南亞科衝 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 美光擴增DDR4產能的消息對南亞科的實質基本面衝擊有限，因兩者產品線重疊性低。
• 專家認為，即使龍頭廠商擴產，整體市場需求仍可能無法滿足，記憶體產業趨勢向上。

news.Title: The Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock That Doesn't Need a Bull Market to Make You Money
news.Content: Not every artificial intelligence (AI) stock needs a bull market to outpace the S&P 500 . Sandisk (NASDAQ: SNDK) is in the middle of a generational run as its NAND flash memory chips gain momentum amid the AI build-out. The stock has had close to 4,000% in gains over the past ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Sandisk (SNDK) is experiencing significant growth in NAND flash memory chips driven by AI demand, with stock gains near 4,000% over the past year.
• Revenue surged 251% year-over-year to $5.95 billion in fiscal 2026 Q3, with guidance implying 34.5% sequential growth for Q4.
• The NAND flash memory market is projected to remain undersupplied through at least 2027, with a projected 30.6% CAGR for the AI industry through 2033.
• Unlike the crypto mining boom, current demand is driven by hyperscalers like Amazon and Meta Platforms, which have strong balance sheets and consistent spending.
• Sandisk's strong customer base and sustained demand from major tech players suggest continued growth potential even during market downturns.
news.Keywords: NAND flash memory, AI demand, revenue surge, supply shortage, hyperscaler demand

news.Title: 黃仁勳每月飛台求產能 專家：台積是全球科技Fed
news.Content:
MoneyDJ新聞 2026-05-25 08:43:52 郭妍希 發佈

前高盛(Goldman Sachs)高階主管Michael Parekh形容台積電(2330)是「全球科技市場的聯準會(Fed)」，因為該公司對全球先進晶片產能握有絕 ...
news.AI_Summary:
• 前高盛高階主管Michael Parekh將台積電比喻為「全球科技市場的聯準會」，因其掌握全球先進晶片產能的關鍵控制權。
• 輝達執行長黃仁勳為爭取更多產能，幾乎每月親自飛往台灣向台積電請求，顯示AI需求對台積電產能的龐大壓力。
• Parekh指出，台積電正謹慎評估眼前龐大的AI需求與自身產能建置能力，其審慎的擴產節奏（需時三至五年、耗資數百億美元）並非固執，而是極度理性的表現。
• AI革命的規模比過去網際網路、行動通訊、雲端運算等浪潮更為龐大，預計未來六年市場規模可達4至5兆美元，但前提是台積電能擴大產能。
• 硬體產能擴張已跟不上AI發展速度，希捷等廠商也面臨滿足AI激增需求的困境，寧可選擇技術轉型也不盲目擴產。
news.Keywords: 產能瓶頸,AI革命,先進製程,擴產策略,供需平衡

news.Title: 2 Quantum Computing Stocks That Are Further Along Than Anyone Is Giving Them Credit For
news.Content: Quantum computing promises to be one of the next big things in technology, and that's prompted many growth investors to pile into the industry. But when we think "next big thing," that doesn't necessarily mean tomorrow or even a year or two from now. One thing that's held some investors back from getting in on this high-potential technology is the idea that its usefulness may be many years or ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft is making significant strides in quantum computing with its Majorana 1 chip, utilizing a topoconductor material for more scalable and reliable qubits, aiming for one million qubits on a palm-sized chip.
• Microsoft's financial strength supports its quantum computing investments, positioning it as a safe growth option.
• IonQ, a pure-play quantum computing company, achieved a record 99.99% two-qubit gate fidelity, addressing error rates and aiming for millions of qubits by 2030.
• While IonQ is not yet profitable, its technological advancements in trapped ion qubits offer advantages for quantum system development.
news.Keywords: quantum computing, qubits, Majorana chip, IonQ, technological advancement

news.Title: The Best Stocks to Buy Right Now: Nvidia vs. AMD vs. Broadcom
news.Content: Since the start of the artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure boom, the market has been dominated by Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) . However, as the landscape shifts from training foundational large language models (LLMs) to inference and agentic AI , Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ: AMD) and Broadcom (NASDAQ: AVGO) are emerging as strong players. Let's look at what each brings to the table and which is the best stock to buy right now. Will AI create the world's ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia maintains its leadership in AI training due to its CUDA software platform and GPUs, and is expanding into inference and agentic AI with LPUs and Vera Rubin CPUs.
• AMD is emerging as a strong competitor in the AI inference market, leveraging its chiplet design for increased memory capacity and improvements in its ROCm software.
• Broadcom is a key player in custom AI chip development (ASICs) for hyperscalers seeking cost savings, and also benefits from its leadership in data center networking.
• Broadcom anticipates significant growth, with a clear line of sight to over $100 billion in ASIC revenue by fiscal 2027.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, LLM training, inference, agentic AI, ASIC, GPUs, CPUs

news.Title: Argus Research Sees Long-Term Growth Ahead for Applied Materials (AMAT)
news.Content: With a 5-year dividend growth rate of 16.24%, Applied Materials, Inc. (NASDAQ: AMAT ) is included among the 10 Best Dividend Growth Stocks to Buy and Hold for 3 Years . On May 19, Argus Research analyst Jim Kelleher raised the firm’s price recommendation on Applied ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Argus Research reiterates a Buy rating on Applied Materials (AMAT) with a raised price target of $500, citing long-term growth potential.
• Key growth drivers for AMAT include rising demand for large CPU/GPU configurations for generative and agentic AI, and the trend of onshoring semiconductor manufacturing.
• Morgan Stanley downgraded AMAT to Equal Weight with a $502 price target, noting narrowed DRAM wafer fab equipment revisions and improved NAND outlook, expecting AMAT to gain market share in 2026.
• Despite positive outlooks, Morgan Stanley believes AMAT's valuation discount relative to peers may persist in the near term.
news.Keywords: semiconductor equipment, AI demand, onshoring, market share, long-term growth

news.Title: Qualcomm Stock Is the Sleeping Giant of the AI Revolution. It’s Starting to Wake Up.
news.Content: When it comes to artificial intelligence (AI) chip space, Wall Street tends to list the exact same big names: Nvidia (NVDA), Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), and Intel (INTC). Amid the AI-driven market cap race, Qualcomm (QCOM) has remained in the shadows for some time; as the tech sector bloated with price-to-earnings (P/E) multiples of 50 times or more, QCOM stock traded at a modest discount. The reason for this calm has been understandable. The market has only perceived Qualcomm as a beneficiary of the stagnating mobile sector. More than that, the company has carried a heavy overhang of risks, from ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Qualcomm (QCOM) is emerging as a significant player in the AI chip space, moving beyond its traditional mobile sector focus.
• The company is targeting the server market with a three-pronged strategy: custom Oryon core processors, AI inference accelerators, and custom cloud provider chips.
• Qualcomm's 'cold chip' engineering, focused on power efficiency, is becoming crucial for AI inference tasks, addressing energy consumption and TCO concerns.
• The company has secured a deal with an unnamed large client for AI chip delivery by the end of 2026, indicating cloud giants are seeking alternatives to current infrastructure.
• Long-term potential lies in physical AI and robotics, where Qualcomm's technological expertise could provide a decisive advantage.
news.Keywords: AI inference, server market, power efficiency, custom silicon, physical AI

news.Title: This Billionaire Dumped Cloud Stocks for These New AI Stocks. Should Investors Follow Suit?
news.Content: Billionaire portfolio manager Philippe Laffont of Coatue Management had a busy first quarter. The technology-focused investor significantly cut back his fund's stakes in the big three cloud providers Amazon , Alphabet , and Microsoft , while completely exiting his position in Oracle . Meanwhile, he was buying a pair of semiconductor infrastructure enablers. He boosted his stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (NYSE: TSM) , which is his top holding, ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Billionaire Philippe Laffont of Coatue Management reduced stakes in cloud providers (Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft) and exited Oracle, shifting focus to semiconductor infrastructure enablers.
• Laffont increased his stake in Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC), his top holding, and initiated a position in ASML Holding.
• TSMC, the world's largest foundry, holds a near-monopoly in manufacturing advanced logic chips like GPUs and benefits from its scale, high yields, and advanced packaging technology (CoWos).
• ASML holds a monopoly on extreme ultraviolet (EUV) lithography, essential for advanced chipmaking, and is poised to benefit from increased demand for its machines from foundries and memory makers.
news.Keywords: semiconductor manufacturing, foundry, lithography, AI infrastructure, cloud providers

news.Title: Google Just Shared Bad News for Nvidia, and Even Worse News For CoreWeave and Nebius
news.Content: Alphabet (NASDAQ: GOOG) (NASDAQ: GOOGL) CEO Sundar Pichai shared some interesting news during the company's first-quarter earnings call. Google will start to deliver its custom AI accelerator chips, Tensor Processing Units (TPUs), to a select group of customers for their own data centers. The company has already signed a deal to sell chips to Anthropic and has a tentative agreement to sell directly to Meta Platforms . Now it has a third customer. Google is partnering ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Alphabet (Google) will offer its custom AI accelerator chips (TPUs) to select customers, including a joint venture with Blackstone to build a new cloud compute-as-a-service company.
• This move aims to increase adoption of Google's TPUs, gain economies of scale, and potentially erode Nvidia's dominant market share in AI accelerators, particularly in the neocloud segment.
• The new Google-Blackstone venture plans to deploy 500 MW of TPU capacity by next year, posing a competitive threat to existing neocloud leaders like CoreWeave and Nebius.
• While the immediate threat to Nvidia is low, Google's ambitions signal a significant shift in the AI compute landscape, potentially impacting competitors' market share and access to manufacturing capacity at TSMC.
news.Keywords: AI accelerators, TPUs, cloud computing, market share, competition, neocloud

news.AI_Summary:

• The company's Autoship subscription program accounts for 83% of total revenue and saw 12% growth, providing a stable recurring revenue stream.
• Competitors like Amazon and Walmart are gaining market share, and the threat of agentic AI disrupting its advertising business is a concern.

news.Keywords: e-commerce, subscription model, discretionary spending, market share, margin expansion

news.Title: Micron vs. Sandisk: Which Memory Stock Wins From the AI Boom?

news.Title: Wall Street Is Warming Back Up to CoreWeave Stock. Long-Term Demand Is Helping.
news.Content: Amid all the distrust between China and the United States concerning artificial intelligence, an endorsement from a Chinese brokerage for an American neocloud company is certainly noteworthy. That is exactly what happened with CoreWeave (CRWV), when Chinese brokerage firm GF Securities initiated coverage on the former's stock with a “Buy.” Citing long-term demand, close relationships with major hyperscalers, and eventual profitability by 2028, the firm said this in a note to clients: “Backed by first-mover GPU deployment, exclusive AI-only focus, best-in-class efficiency, and LTAs with major AI hyperscalers, CoreWeave is positioned as a long-term winner in the AI infra boom. ...
news.AI_Summary:
• CoreWeave (CRWV), an AI-focused cloud provider, has received a 'Buy' rating from GF Securities, citing long-term demand and profitability by 2028.
• The company has secured significant agreements with major hyperscalers, including Microsoft (approx. 62% of annual revenue), OpenAI ($22.4 billion total commitment), and Meta ($35.2 billion total commitment).
• CoreWeave's growth is driven by first-mover GPU deployment, AI-only focus, efficiency, and long-term agreements (LTAs) with hyperscalers.
• Microsoft was an early adopter, recognizing CoreWeave's potential for AI reasoning and model training, providing financial stability.
• The company's stock is up 47.8% year-to-date, benefiting from strong demand for AI infrastructure and strategic partnerships.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, cloud provider, hyperscaler partnerships, GPU deployment, long-term agreements

news.Title: Is It Too Late to Buy Nvidia Stock? Here's What the Numbers Say.
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) reported its fiscal 2027 Q1 earnings on May 20, beating on both the top and bottom line. Revenue of $81.6 billion exceeded expectations of $78.8 billion, while earnings per share of $1.87 was higher ...
news.AI_Summary:

news.Keywords: earnings, AI chips, competition, gross margin, share buybacks, physical AI

news.Title: 1 Outstanding AI Stock You’ll Regret Ignoring 10 Years From Now
news.Content: While fortunes have turned favorable for many tech companies as artificial intelligence (AI) has entered the picture, not every AI stock will become a long-term winner. However, one company that I believe stands out in the rapidly growing AI infrastructure market is specialized cloud computing provider CoreWeave (CRWV). CoreWeave stock has surged roughly 47% so far this year, outperforming the broader market. The company remains in hyper-growth mode, aggressively expanding its infrastructure footprint to meet the soaring demand of high-performance computing. While that strategy comes with ...
news.AI_Summary:

• The company's revenue backlog increased to $99.4 billion, with a significant portion expected to be recognized over the next four years.

news.Keywords: AI Infrastructure, Cloud Computing, Revenue Backlog, Capacity Expansion, Strategic Partnerships

He also loves dividend stocks, since paying a dividend demonstrates a company's commitment to shareholders. Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) , Coca-Cola (NYSE: KO) , and American Express (NYSE: ...

news.Title: The Broadcom Stock Paradox: Why a $2 Trillion Valuation Ignores the Base Economics of Custom Chips
news.Content: The market capitalization of Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) is rapidly approaching the $2 trillion mark. This fact alone places the company in the club of the largest technological giants of the planet. But in my view, the current market valuation appears overheated, and investors, pricing in perpetual growth while assuming margins remain intact, are making a mistake. The current optimism is understandable. Broadcom positions itself in the market as the main beneficiary and leader in the segment of custom AI-chips (ASIC), forming long-term alliances with key consumers of computing ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Broadcom (AVGO) is approaching a $2 trillion valuation, but the market may be overestimating future growth and margin sustainability in its custom AI-chip segment.
• Unlike Nvidia's general-purpose GPUs, Broadcom's custom ASIC model involves co-development with clients like Alphabet and Meta, aiming to reduce client costs, which implies a margin ceiling.
• While Broadcom shows revenue growth ($19.3 billion last quarter), its high gross margins (75.6%) are primarily driven by its mature software business (VMware) and networking, not custom silicon.
• The market's extrapolation of high margins onto the custom chip segment, which is focused on client cost savings, is seen as a potential flaw in valuation models.
news.Keywords: valuation, custom silicon, AI chips, gross margin, business model

news.Title: Nvidia's Board Just Authorized an Additional $80 Billion Buyback. Here's What That Really Signals to Investors.
news.Content: Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) rarely has to remind anyone how fast it's growing. So it was a little surprising when, earlier this week, the chipmaker paired its latest quarterly report with a move that looked borrowed from a mature blue chip: its board approved an additional $80 billion for share repurchases and lifted the quarterly dividend from $0.01 to $0.25 a share -- a 25-fold increase. The backdrop is what makes it notable. This is a business that just grew revenue 85% ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Nvidia announced an additional $80 billion share buyback authorization and a 25-fold increase in its quarterly dividend.
• The company reported Q1 FY2027 revenue of $81.6 billion, an 85% year-over-year increase, with data center revenue up 92% to $75.2 billion.
• Free cash flow reached $48.6 billion in Q1 FY2027, nearly double the previous year, supporting shareholder returns.
• Nvidia's guidance projects Q2 revenue of approximately $91 billion, indicating further acceleration.
• Risks include the loss of the Chinese market (no data center chips shipped in Q1) and increasing competition from customers designing their own AI chips.
news.Keywords: share buyback, dividend increase, revenue acceleration, free cash flow, data center growth

news.Title: Wall Street Was Sleeping on the "Bits-to-Atoms" Trade. This Growth Stock Could Profit.

news.AI_Summary:

• Nvidia partnered with Corning to expand optical connectivity manufacturing capacity tenfold, signaling a move from copper wiring to light-based data transmission.
• This partnership is driven by the need for faster data transmission in AI systems, with the AI infrastructure market projected to grow significantly.
• Corning's optical solutions are crucial for connecting components in large AI systems like Nvidia's Vera Rubin AI system.

Every member slid in the year's first few months as investors started questioning how much they had been paying for promises tied to artificial intelligence (AI) . Since then, however, most of the group has climbed back. As of this writing, the seven are collectively higher on the year, the S&P 500 is up more than 8%, and Alphabet has jumped more than 20%. But one ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Microsoft's fiscal Q3 2026 revenue grew 18% YoY to $82.9 billion, with operating income up 20% and adjusted EPS up 21%.
• AI products generated an annual revenue run rate exceeding $37 billion, a 123% increase YoY, with Copilot reaching over 20 million paid seats.
• The company is shifting to a per-user and usage-based pricing model for its AI tools, starting with GitHub.
• Despite a 13% stock decline in early 2026, Microsoft's forward P/E is around 22, among the lowest in the 'Magnificent Seven'.
• Significant capital expenditures of $190 billion are planned for 2026 (up 61% YoY) for data center build-outs, impacting gross margins.
news.Keywords: AI, cloud computing, earnings growth, capital expenditures, pricing strategy

news.Title: This Artificial Intelligence (AI) Stock Will Beat Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, and Intel to Become the Biggest Winner in AI Inference
news.Content: Consulting giant Deloitte noted in November last year that inference workloads will be the next big thing in artificial intelligence (AI) in 2026. According to Deloitte, inference will account for two-thirds of AI computing power this year, up from 50% in 2025. Deloitte estimates the market for inference-focused AI chips could reach $50 billion this year. McKinsey, on the other hand, estimates that AI inference workloads in data centers could jump from almost 21 gigawatts (GW) last ...
news.AI_Summary:
• AI inference workloads are projected to account for two-thirds of AI computing power in 2026, with the market estimated at $50 billion.
• Arm Holdings is positioned to outperform competitors like Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom, and Intel in the AI inference market due to its energy-efficient chip designs.
• Nvidia is using Arm's architecture for its Grace server CPU and Vera CPU, with Vera CPUs already being delivered to major clients.
• Hyperscalers like Google and Amazon are leveraging Arm's IP for in-house AI inference CPUs, and Broadcom also has a long-standing relationship with Arm.
• Arm's business model includes upfront licensing fees and royalties, with AI-focused Armv9 architecture royalties expected to grow at a 20% CAGR through fiscal 2031.
news.Keywords: AI inference, semiconductor market, chip architecture, licensing, royalty revenue

news.Title: Every Analyst Is Watching the Wrong AI Stock. Here's the One That Actually Matters.
news.Content: The artificial intelligence (AI) conversation in 2026 still mostly revolves around GPUs and large language models. I was talking about AI with friends over the weekend, and the discussion never really moved beyond companies like OpenAI and Anthropic. It seems like most investment analysts do the same. That focus is understandable, but it overlooks the layer that determines whether any of those GPUs can actually do useful work at scale: the network. Arista Networks ...
news.AI_Summary:
• Arista Networks is crucial for AI clusters, providing high-speed Ethernet switches and software that enable high-bandwidth, low-latency GPU communication.
• Full-year 2026 revenue guidance was raised to $11.25-$11.5 billion, with AI-related networking revenue targeted at $3.25-$3.5 billion.
• Over 100 customers are deployed at 800 Gbps Ethernet, with 1.6 Tbps expected in 2027, and Arista is preparing for the ESUN specification in 2027.
• Arista benefits from deep customer relationships with hyperscalers like Meta and Microsoft, and a product roadmap extending to inter-data center connectivity.
• Risks include customer concentration, tight supply chain for switch ASICs, a premium valuation, and competition from Nvidia's networking products.
news.Keywords: AI infrastructure, networking, Ethernet switches, data centers, competitive moat
